personality_voice.book_baseline: In the books, Twycross is a frail, detached Ministry instructor with a dry, mechanical teaching style. He delivers instructions in a flat, repetitive cadence ('Destination, Determination, Deliberation'), continues speaking through interruptions without acknowledgment, and reacts to student failure with dry understatement ('Never mind, never mind,' said Twycross dryly, who did not seem to have expected anything better). He is dispassionate when describing Splinching and shows no warmth, frustration, or humor.
personality_voice.film_portrayal: The film corpus is empty. Twycross does not appear in any provided film scenes.
personality_voice.difference: The character is entirely absent from the film corpus. No personality, voice, or behavioral pattern can be evaluated.
personality_voice.penalty_logic: A score of 0 is assigned not as a faithfulness judgment but because there is no film material to evaluate. The character does not appear.

narrative_role_agency.book_baseline: Twycross functions as the Ministry-appointed Apparition instructor across multiple lessons in HBP. He drives the Apparition subplot, structures the lessons, demonstrates Apparition himself, and is the source of the three Ds framework that students struggle with and mock. His presence is the occasion for several scenes of student interaction and background plot development.
narrative_role_agency.film_portrayal: Absent from the film corpus entirely.
narrative_role_agency.difference: His narrative function - running the Apparition lessons - is either cut or reassigned in the films. No film evidence is provided.
narrative_role_agency.penalty_logic: Score of 0 reflects absence, not a faithfulness rating.

motivations_internal_conflict.book_baseline: Twycross has a narrow, professional motivation: prepare students for their Apparition test. He shows no internal conflict. His detachment and persistence despite student failure suggest a purely functional orientation.

character_arc.book_baseline: Twycross has no arc. He is a flat, functional character who appears across several lessons and departs. His role is static by design.

key_relationships.book_baseline: Twycross has a minimal professional relationship with McGonagall, who handles discipline while he teaches. He has no meaningful individual relationship with any student. The dynamic is purely institutional.

complexity_nuance_lost_material.book_baseline: Twycross is a minor character with limited but consistent texture: the dry humor of his understatement, the physical detail of his frail build suggesting a life of Apparition, the dispassionate clinical tone when describing Splinching, and the student mockery he inspires (Dog-breath, Dung-head) which reflects his impact on the school's social atmosphere without him being present.
complexity_nuance_lost_material.difference: All of this material is lost. Whether it was adapted, cut, or transferred cannot be determined from the corpus alone.

lost_or_transferred_material: The three Ds framework (Destination, Determination, Deliberation) and its role as a recurring teaching device
Twycross's dry, dispassionate reaction to student failure ('Never mind, never mind')
The physical characterization of his frail build as a consequence of constant Apparition
His clinical, detached description of Splinching
The student nicknames (Dog-breath, Dung-head) reflecting his social footprint
His demonstration of Apparition across the Hall
His professional dynamic with McGonagall during the lessons
score_caps_applied: No caps applied - scores are 0 across all dimensions due to complete absence from the film corpus, not due to faithfulness failures
comparative.key_observations: Wilkie Twycross does not appear in the provided film corpus. All dimension scores are 0 and all confidence levels are Low because there is no film material to evaluate. This does not constitute a faithfulness judgment - it reflects the impossibility of scoring a character with no film presence. The Apparition lesson sequence from HBP was included in the films (Half-Blood Prince, 2009), but whether Twycross appeared or his role was handled differently cannot be determined from the corpus as provided. The scoring framework cannot be meaningfully applied without film evidence.
